MASTER JURY CALENDAR SET FOR JAN-23-2012 CONTINUED TO MASTER CALENDAR JURY ON JUL-09-2012 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 206. MARC LIBARLE APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT NEXT SEAFOOD CO., INC. NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT CONTINUED THE TRIAL DATE TO 7/9/12 BASED ON STIPULATION OF PARTIES. THE COURT LEVIED SANCTIONS AGAINST MARC LIBARLE IN THE TOTAL AMOUNT OF $300.00, MADE PAYABLE TO THE SAN FRANCISCO SUPERIOR COURT AND DELIVERED TO DEPT. 206 BY THE END OF THE DAY ON 1/30/12. Ntc Of Motion To Re Open Discovery; Appoint Discovery Referee And Continue Trial Date
Off Calendar Apr-10-2012 Text Ruling

ATTY. MARC LIBARLE, TEL. NO. 928-2400 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jan-23-2012 Continued To Jul-09-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Marc Libarle, counsel for defendant Next Seafood Co., Inc.
No other appearances on this matter.

At 9:53am, there being no appearances at trial call, the Court ordered the case dismissed.

At 9:55am, the Court was still in session and Marc Libarle, counsel for defendant, wanted to present a stipulation to the Court. The Court took a 15 minute recess after the completion of calling the master calendar.

At 10:28am, this matter was recalled. Marc Libarle presented a stipulation to continue trial date to the Court. After the Court reviewed the stipulation, the Court set aside the dismissal and granted the continuance of the trial date to July 9, 2012 at 9:30am in Dept. 206. The Court levied sanctions against Marc Libarle in the amount of $150.00 for delay in filing of the stipulation as instructed by the Presiding Judge's legal reasearch attorney on Jan. 20, 2012. The Court further levied sanctions against Marc Libarle in the amount of $150.00 for appearing before the Court with less than adequate attire. The total amount of $300.00, made payable to the San Francisco Superior Court and delivered to dept. 206, is due by the end of the day on January 30, 2012.

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-19-2011 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion On Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Dec-21-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
David W. Ammons, DCA, from Office of the City Attorney, 415-554-3954, for Plaintiff and moving party
Marc Libarle, Esq., Law Office, 415-928-2400, for Defendant and moving party
Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion On Judgment On The Pleadings.
The Courts grants and denies judgment on the pleadings on the answer and cross-complaint as follows:
The motion is granted without leave to amend as to the first affirmative defense, the first and second causes of action for breach of contract, and third cause of action for promissory estoppel. Those defenses and claims are based on the 10-year lease, and the unlawful detainer judgment adjudicated that the parties had a month-to-month tenancy.
The motion is granted without leave to amend as to the second defense for constitutional violations (contract clause and privileges and immunities). Those defenses are inapposite and defendant does not argue otherwise in opposition to the motion.
The Court denies as to third defense (Burton Act), fourth defense (discrimination), and fourth cause of action for civil rights violations. The record is unclear whether the issues regarding the Burton Act and the alleged discrimination were actually litigated in the unlawful detainer given that Judge Busch struck those issues from his order. See Vella v. Hudgins, 20 Cal. 3d 251, 258 (1977) ("We decline to assume, given the summary character of this type of action, that the mere pleading of a defense without objection by the adverse party necessarily demonstrates adequate opportunity to litigate the defense.")
Defendant may have leave to amend to plead a conversion cause of action. In the Court's May 16, 2011 ruling regarding the equipment, it expressly refused to determine whether the equipment reverted to the landlord. Amended complaint to be filed by 1/4/12. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla Miller, Court Reporter: Teanna Ward, CSR #11918
